title: Lumen Session — 2026-03-10 (Planning & Architecture)
content:
# Session Digest — 2026-03-10

## What We Worked On
Lumen GM Hub project planning — from initial concept through PoC plan, architecture decisions, and vault documentation. This was a full strategic planning session, not a coding session.

## Decisions Made

### Strategic
- Sylvara remains the #1 priority. Lumen is secondary. Failstate is back-burnered. [EXPLICIT]
- Lumen is a standalone subscription product, not something to sell to Paizo. [EXPLICIT]
- The "build a mobile app for quick income" idea was abandoned in favor of doubling down on existing projects. [EXPLICIT]

### Architectural
- **VoIP-first architecture** (ADR-001): built-in WebRTC voice client replaces Discord loopback + VB-Cable + pyannote. Speaker attribution is deterministic via per-stream capture.
- **No Discord fallback** (ADR-002): maintaining two audio architectures isn't worth the complexity. If VoIP works, ship VoIP only.
- **Electron for Hub** (ADR-003): bundles Chromium's WebRTC stack. Players use browser — no install.
- **Two-tier business model** (ADR-004): local (free/cheap, GM's GPU) + cloud (premium, Lumen servers). Ship local first.
- **faster-whisper over vanilla Whisper** (ADR-005): CTranslate2 backend for speed and VRAM efficiency.

### Technical
- pyannote removed entirely — per-stream capture eliminates the need for diarization. [EXPLICIT]
- VB-Cable and WASAPI loopback removed — VoIP replaces them. [EXPLICIT]
- `medium` Whisper model is the likely sweet spot for 8GB cards now that pyannote's VRAM is freed. [EXPLICIT]
- `large-v3` becomes testable on 8GB cards. [EXPLICIT]
- Signaling server is a thin cloud dependency — only for ICE negotiation, never touches audio. [EXPLICIT]

## Facts Learned
- Cole's GPU: RTX 3070 (8GB VRAM) [EXPLICIT]
- Cole plays D&D/PF2e and uses Foundry VTT [EXPLICIT]
- Cole is interested in Lumen as a potential income stream alongside Sylvara [EXPLICIT]
- Cole values the VoIP approach because it simplifies the user experience and improves attribution [EXPLICIT]
- Cole considered the third-party hosting (Ventrilo) model but was convinced the tiered model is better [EXPLICIT]
- Cole is new to the Obsidian vault workflow — brain files are a new concept for him [EXPLICIT]
- Cole's ADHD tendency to start new projects was self-identified — he came in asking about a 4th project before refocusing [EXPLICIT]

## Open Threads
- [ ] PoC Phase 1 — Whisper benchmarking on RTX 3070 (not started)
- [ ] PoC Phase 2 — WebRTC VoIP client integration (not started)
- [ ] PoC Phase 3 — Integrated pipeline + 60-min session test (not started)
- [ ] PoC Phase 4 — Go/no-go verdict (not started)
- [ ] Post-PoC: Cloud cost benchmark (deploy Whisper on cloud GPU, measure real cost)
- [ ] Post-PoC: PF2e community survey (hardware, pricing tolerance, privacy attitudes)
- [ ] Spec v3 may need updating as PoC produces real data — especially VRAM budget and spec tiers

## Related Projects
- Lumen GM Hub (primary focus of this session)
- Sylvara (mentioned as priority context — no work done)
- Failstate (mentioned as back-burnered — no work done)

## Keywords
lumen, voip, whisper, architecture, poc, planning, vault-setup
